title: Daisy's Tiny Dog Birthday Adventure
summary: Bluey needs help preparing a massive birthday feast for her friend Daisy, a tiny dog who is turning five years old. Enlisting the help of Izzy the Kiddo, they search through a magical Lego bin for flower treats and bananas before heading to the grocery store to stock up on crunchy, chocolate-flavored soda and an assortment of fruits and vegetables. After a quick trip through the checkout line, they decorate the house with fire trucks and prepare for a house full of friends, including Queso, Bingo, and the rest of the pack. With a five-candle cake and a chorus of barks, the party celebrates Daisy's big day in style.
audioFilename: * Stars and stories - Daisy Dog’s Birthday.m4a

highlightQuote.text: It's crunchy and sweet and it has chocolate flavored.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Alright, I'm going to set the timer for Stars and Stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But I need this much time.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, well let me just adjust it to how much time we're actually going to do, which is more like this. That's still a lot of time. And we'll put it right up here so we can see it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then we'll turn off the big light, and we'll turn off the other light. And now the stars are ready and it's time for Stars and Stories!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, you can tell a story about whatever you want it to be about. With washing machine junk, or jellyfish, or octopus, or Peppa, or anyone else that wants to be in your story.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, I'll give you Pep. You could tell a story about or you could tell a story about Phil or Daniel or Rachel or Bluey. You could tell a story about anyone that you like tell a story about. Dad, which story do you pick?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. How about we tell a story about Pout-Pout fish?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, no, I pick a story about Bluey!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I don't know about Bluey. Tell me about Bluey.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But you have to make up a story about him and then you'll remember what Bluey is like.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I've never watched Bluey. Is Bluey a dog?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, Bluey is a dog. And let's tell a story about him.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, well once upon a time, Bluey said to Izzy the Kiddo, 'Izzy, I need your help because...'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What? Because what Bluey?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Because I'm going to have a party for somebody. It's somebody's birthday. Whose birthday is it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's your friend Bingo's birthday. Actually it's Queso's birthday, no actually it's Daisy's birthday. It's Daisy's birthday, she's also a dog and she's a tiny dog.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Bluey said, 'It's my friend Daisy the tiny dog's birthday, and a lot of people are invited to celebrate.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: How about Queso?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Queso's coming, and we need to make a delicious meal, a feast for everyone, but I need your help to cook it. Can you help me make the meal for the party?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But I don't have any food up here.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, maybe we can get some. How could we get some food?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: By searching in the Lego bin for a banana for everyone.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Look, some nice game. Look, some nice game. Bluey, I just found some nice game and I found something for to eat. Maybe you just eat small flower treats.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, some flower treats and some bananas.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, and there's the banana. Oh, you're cutting up the banana, thank you. Yeah, and I already cut it. So now you can have lots of flowers you want in your special games, and there's banana, and there's flowers, special dice games. Now Bluey, I made a party for everyone!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, I think we need a little bit more though. Hmm, I think we should serve some more food. Let's go to the grocery store and buy some more food.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But I don't have a grocery store, sorry Bluey.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that's okay Izzy the Kiddo. We can drive over to the grocery store, it's right nearby. And so, Izzy...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Where?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It's just around the corner. Over there. Over there. And Bluey got in the car and they drove drive drive drive drive. And then they got out of the car, and then they were in the grocery store. And Bluey said, 'Hmm, I don't know what to pick out.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know what, well maybe we should get some some soda.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Some soda? What kind of soda?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: This kind of soda. I can break a little piece for you to try.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, thanks. Look look. Um, Bluey would you like this small piece of soda?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Sure. What flavor is it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's crunchy and sweet and it has chocolate flavored.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, that sounds delicious. I'll try it. Mmm, let's buy some for the party. Okay, what else?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I already bought some.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, good.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I have lots more if you need more. Handing out more. More soda. And this is the soda crack that we're already so getting.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then I know what else we...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Here, we should get some some fruit for the party. What kind of fruit should we get?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: How about orange? Lemonade? And watermelon?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that sounds great. Okay, I got some of each of those. How about some vegetables? What should we get for vegetables?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: How about sour cream?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Sour cream and radish and this is lemonade, sour cream and the this the camera rolled.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The camera rolled and this is the the cream. The nice sweet cream and this is the lemonade. Look Bluey, I'm helping you make your party.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Thank you so much. All right, let's get one last thing. What's one more thing we can buy for food for the party?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Some orange. And some some lemon-y. Hmm, how about more bluebur-dose? How about more radish? That's all the things that we need to buy.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, some radish. Okay, that sounds great. All right.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And we need a pack for our stuff in case we're done with it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, we'll pack it up in there. All right, we'll take it over to the place where we check out, and the nice cashier will go beep beep beep and ring everything up and then we'll pay.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then we'll bring it over to our car and bring it back to my house, said Bluey. And now it's almost time for the party. Izzy, do you need to go pee-pee?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I did. I'm mixed. I pretended I was the cashier.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, thanks. Okay, beep beep beep. All right, now it's almost time for the party. Um, let's get out some decorations. How should we decorate?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, I have two pins of decorations and I also have one more decoration which is a fire truck.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that sounds nice.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We should put our fish, or this, or even anything that you want.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I hear someone barking outside. Bark bark bark. I think that's Daisy the dog and it's her birthday.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Queso and...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and Queso's coming too. Bark bark bark bark. And who else is coming over?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Your friend Bingo, and your mommy dog Rob, and Blue-oh her dad, and Bingo, and Bingo, and Pringo, and Tringo, and Fingo.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, we have so many wonderful guests for our party for Daisy's birthday. Okay, come in everyone! And everyone says hello hello hello hello hello hello. And Bluey says, oh and by the way, I got a beautiful little birthday cake for Daisy dog too.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Look my I have some decorations.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, look at those beautiful decorations, says Daisy dog. Thank you for decorating for my party. Oh, and all of this beautiful food. There's salad greens and sour cream...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And there's nice games and there's this little pack with food.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And some nice watermelon and blueberries and lemonade and soda. What an amazing party! And then Bluey said, okay, now it's time for the cake.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Bluey turned out the light and lit the candles for Daisy's birthday. How old is Daisy turning?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Five years old.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, Daisy's five years old. And so Bluey lit five candles on the cake and then everyone sang. Ready? Happy birthday to Daisy dog, happy birthday to you. Happy birthday dear Daisy dog, happy birthday dear Daisy dog.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Izzy said bark bark bark and blew out all five candles and then everyone said yay! Yay! Okay, and now we can eat our snacks and have some cake and play some games.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And oh, that's our time's up for telling the story. But we got to the end because that was the end of the wonderful birthday party that Izzy the Kiddo helped to make for Daisy the dog. The end.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now let's put all this decoration back.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Good idea, thank you.

characters.name: Bluey
characters.description: A dog who is hosting a big birthday party for a friend.

characters.name: Izzy the Kiddo
characters.description: Once a quiet dreamer sketching from a recovery bed, this brave explorer now leads her companions through Hattie’s workshop and the lightless abyss, guided by a beacon of special light. Clad in a rugged driving suit alongside Bluey and Fincat’s Mouse, she wields heavy hammers and a magic wand with logistical precision inherited from her Nana. From trading plastic bricks for rare mushrooms to orchestrating neighborhood cleanups, she balances specialized gear with a playful spirit. Choosing the spark of a game over the gravity of a traditional quest, she ensures her radiant creativity and guiding light remain her most powerful tools for navigating the unknown.

characters.name: Daisy
characters.description: A tiny dog who is the guest of honor at her fifth birthday party.

characters.name: Queso
characters.description: A dog friend invited to the birthday party feast.

characters.name: Bingo
characters.description: A friend of Bluey and one of the many birthday party guests.

characters.name: Mommy
characters.description: A dog guest who comes to celebrate Daisy's birthday.

characters.name: Bandit
characters.description: Bluey's dad who joins the group for the party.

characters.name: Pringo
characters.description: A canine friend invited to the birthday celebration.

characters.name: Tringo
characters.description: A dog who arrives at the house to celebrate with Daisy.

characters.name: Fingo
characters.description: A diligent mole scuba diver who maintains the iridescent rainbow fish habitat, now surfacing from his chores to join another dog guest in a boisterous chorus of friends singing "Happy Birthday" to the sea life.

places.name: Lego Bin
places.description: A place where Izzy finds imaginative food items like flower treats and bananas.

places.name: Grocery Store
places.description: A distant neighborhood shop stocked with soda, crisp celery, and jewel-toned pomegranates, forever etched in the trio’s memory for the bananas they gathered and the messy remains of squished raspberries. It remains a whimsical local sanctuary where, amidst the nostalgia, even the resident mice can be found purchasing small bowls of hearty vegetable soup.

places.name: Bluey's House
places.description: The festive location where the birthday feast and party are held.
